name: Spring MVC
description: Spring’s annotation‑driven Model‑View‑Controller framework for building web apps.

name: Apache Struts
description: Classic action‑based MVC framework originally from Apache.

name: Apache Struts 2
description: Successor to Struts 1 with annotation and plugin system.

name: Jakarta Faces
description: Specification and reference implementation for component‑based server‑side UI.

name: Apache Wicket
description: Component‑oriented Java web framework emphasizing stateful pages.

name: Apache Tapestry
description: Modular, annotation‑driven component web framework.

name: Play Framework
description: Reactive, stateless MVC framework with hot‑reload.

name: Grails
description: Groovy‑powered, Spring‑based rapid web application framework.

name: Dropwizard
description: Opinionated stack for lean RESTful services combining Jetty, Jersey and metrics.

name: Micronaut
description: Ahead‑of‑time compiled, cloud‑native framework with fast startup.

name: Quarkus
description: Kubernetes‑native Java stack tuned for GraalVM and fast JVM startup.

name: Helidon
description: Oracle‑backed microservice framework offering SE and MP flavours.

name: Spark Java
description: Tiny Sinatra‑like framework for quick REST APIs.

name: JFinal
description: Chinese minimalist MVC framework with ActiveRecord support.

name: Ninja Framework
description: Full‑stack web framework inspired by Ruby on Rails.

name: Restlet
description: First Java framework fully aligned with REST architectural style.

name: Apache Click
description: Page‑oriented web framework emphasizing simplicity and velocity templates.

name: Ratpack
description: Non‑blocking, Groovy/Java microframework built on Netty.

name: Vert.x Web
description: Vert.x toolkit extension providing routing and MVC helpers.

name: Vaadin
description: Server‑side Java UI framework generating modern web components.

name: Apache Sling
description: RESTful web framework for content‑oriented applications atop JCR.

name: Google Web Toolkit
description: Compiles Java into optimized JavaScript for rich web apps.

name: JavaFX
description: Modern Java client UI toolkit with hardware‑accelerated graphics.

name: Swing
description: Original Java SE widget toolkit for desktop GUIs.

name: SWT
description: Eclipse’s native‑widget desktop toolkit.

name: Apache Pivot
description: Declarative UI toolkit for rich internet applications.

name: PrimeFaces
description: Popular JSF component library with rich widgets.

name: RichFaces
description: Red Hat’s Ajax‑enabled JSF component suite.

name: ICEfaces
description: Enterprise JSF framework delivering Ajax push and mobile components.

name: SwingX
description: Community extension adding modern widgets to Swing.

name: Spring Framework
description: Comprehensive programming model with powerful dependency injection.

name: Google Guice
description: Lightweight, annotation‑driven dependency injection library.

name: Dagger
description: Compile‑time dependency injection framework from Google.

name: Weld
description: Reference implementation of Jakarta CDI.

name: PicoContainer
description: Very small inversion‑of‑control container.

name: HK2
description: Jigsaw‑ready injection kernel used by GlassFish.

name: Seasar2
description: Japanese lightweight container influencing Spring annotations.

name: Hibernate ORM
description: Industry‑standard object‑relational mapping framework.

name: MyBatis
description: SQL‑mapping framework giving full control over queries.

name: EclipseLink
description: Jakarta Persistence reference implementation derived from TopLink.

name: Apache OpenJPA
description: Apache’s open‑source JPA implementation.

name: Apache Cayenne
description: Full‑featured ORM and persistence services server.

name: DataNucleus
description: JDO, JPA and Jakarta Data implementation supporting many datastores.

name: jOOQ
description: Typesafe SQL‑building library generating Java DSL from schema.

name: Apache Torque
description: Older code‑generation ORM producing POJOs from DB schema.

name: Apache OJB
description: ObjectRelationalBridge providing configurable mapping descriptors.

name: Morphia
description: MongoDB object‑mapping framework for Java.

name: Kundera
description: JPA‑compliant polyglot ORM for NoSQL databases.

name: Oracle TopLink
description: Commercial pioneer ORM later open‑sourced as EclipseLink.

name: iBatis
description: Predecessor to MyBatis mapping SQL to objects.

name: Project Reactor
description: Reactive Streams implementation powering Spring WebFlux.

name: RxJava
description: Observable sequence library bringing ReactiveX to Java.

name: Eclipse Vert.x
description: Toolkit for building polyglot reactive applications on the JVM.

name: Akka
description: Toolkit and runtime for building concurrent, distributed, resilient actors.

name: Akka Streams
description: Streaming API on top of Akka actors complying with Reactive Streams.

name: Lagom
description: Opinionated microservice framework built on Akka and Play.

name: Netty
description: Asynchronous event‑driven network application framework.

name: Mutiny
description: Small‑footprint reactive programming library used by Quarkus.

name: Quasar
description: Fibers and channels framework bringing lightweight threads to Java.

name: Spring Security
description: Comprehensive authentication and authorization framework for Spring apps.

name: Apache Shiro
description: Easy‑to‑use security framework covering authc, authz, cryptography and session.

name: Keycloak
description: Open‑source identity and access management server from Red Hat.

name: pac4j
description: Generic security engine supporting numerous protocols and clients.

name: JAAS
description: Built‑in Java Authentication and Authorization Service API.

name: Android SDK
description: Official toolkit and libraries for building Android apps in Java/Kotlin.

name: Codename One
description: Write once, deploy native mobile apps with Java.

name: RoboVM
description: AOT compiler for running Java apps natively on iOS.

name: Gluon Mobile
description: JavaFX‑based framework for cross‑platform mobile UIs.

name: LWUIT
description: Lightweight UI Toolkit for Java ME devices.

name: Java ME SDK
description: Toolkit for developing Java Micro Edition applications.

name: LibGDX
description: Cross‑platform game development framework with OpenGL bindings.

name: jMonkeyEngine
description: Scene‑graph‑based 3D game engine for modern Java.

name: LWJGL
description: Low‑level Java binding for OpenGL, Vulkan and OpenAL.

name: Slick2D
description: 2D game library built on LWJGL.

name: Ardor3D
description: Advanced high‑performance 3D engine forked from jME.

name: PlayN
description: Write once, deploy HTML5, desktop and mobile games.

name: BDX
description: Lightweight 3D game framework integrated with Blender.

name: Apache Maven
description: Declarative project management and build automation tool.

name: Gradle
description: Flexible build system with Groovy/Kotlin DSL and incremental execution.

name: Apache Ant
description: XML‑based build tool predating Maven.

name: SBT
description: Interactive Scala/Java build tool with fast incremental compiler.

name: Bazel
description: Google’s fast, reproducible multi‑language build system.

name: Buck
description: Meta’s opinionated build tool focused on caching and parallelism.

name: Pants
description: Monorepo‑oriented build system with Python rule engine and JVM support.

name: Apache Ivy
description: Dependency manager often used with Ant.

name: Buildr
description: Ruby‑based build system for Java projects following Maven conventions.

name: JUnit
description: De‑facto standard unit testing framework for Java.

name: TestNG
description: Feature‑rich testing framework inspired by JUnit and NUnit.

name: Spock
description: Groovy‑based BDD unit and integration testing framework.

name: Mockito
description: Popular mocking framework enabling fluent stubbing and verification.

name: EasyMock
description: Class‑based mock object framework for Java.

name: PowerMock
description: Extension enabling mocking of static, final and private methods.

name: JMockit
description: Coverage‑oriented mocking toolkit allowing full isolation.

name: Arquillian
description: Container‑controlled integration testing platform for Jakarta EE.

name: Cucumber-JVM
description: Behavior‑driven development framework executing Gherkin specs.

name: Serenity BDD
description: Reporting‑focused acceptance testing framework extending JUnit and Cucumber.

name: Spring Cloud
description: Collection of tools for distributed systems patterns on Spring.

name: Apache Brooklyn
description: Model‑driven application blueprinting and autoscaling platform.

name: Netflix Hystrix
description: Latency and fault‑tolerance library implementing circuit breaker pattern.

name: Apache Camel
description: Enterprise Integration Patterns implementation with 300+ connectors.

name: Mule ESB
description: Lightweight enterprise service bus and integration platform.

name: WSO2 ESB
description: Open‑source enterprise service bus based on Synapse.

name: Drools
description: Business rule management system and forward‑chaining inference engine.

name: OptaPlanner
description: AI constraint solver for optimizing planning problems.

name: OSGi
description: Dynamic module system and service platform for the JVM.

name: Apache Karaf
description: OSGi‑based lightweight container supporting hot deployment.

name: Apache Hadoop MapReduce
description: Distributed batch computation framework for large datasets.

name: Apache Storm
description: Distributed real‑time computation system operating on streams.

name: Apache Flink
description: Stateful stream and batch data processing engine with exactly‑once guarantees.

name: Python
description: High-level, interpreted language known for its readability and vast ecosystem. Dominant in data science, ML, scripting, and web backends.

name: JavaScript
description: The language of the web. Runs in every browser and, via Node.js, on the server. ECMAScript standard evolves yearly.

name: TypeScript
description: Strongly-typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft. Compiles to plain JS and adds static type checking to large codebases.

name: Rust
description: Systems programming language guaranteeing memory safety without a garbage collector via its ownership model. High performance with no runtime overhead.

name: Go
description: Statically typed, compiled language from Google. Designed for simplicity, fast compilation, and built-in concurrency primitives (goroutines, channels).

name: Kotlin
description: Modern JVM language by JetBrains, fully interoperable with Java. Official language for Android development and a concise alternative on the server.

name: Swift
description: Apple's modern compiled language replacing Objective-C for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms. Also runs on Linux for server-side use.

name: C#
description: Strongly-typed, object-oriented language from Microsoft running on the .NET runtime. Widely used in enterprise software, game development (Unity), and web.

name: Ruby
description: Dynamic, expressive language optimised for developer happiness. Powers Ruby on Rails and a rich ecosystem of gems. Widely used in startups and web development.

name: PHP
description: Server-side scripting language that powers a large share of the web, including WordPress. Modern PHP 8.x has strong typing, JIT compilation, and fibers.

name: Dart
description: Client-optimised language by Google, the backbone of the Flutter framework. Compiles to native ARM, x64, and JavaScript.

name: Scala
description: Hybrid functional/OOP language on the JVM. Strongly typed with type inference, pattern matching, and a powerful collection library. Powers big-data tools like Spark.

name: Elixir
description: Functional language built on the Erlang VM (BEAM), designed for fault-tolerant, distributed, low-latency systems. Phoenix framework powers real-time web apps.

name: Haskell
description: Purely functional language with lazy evaluation, strong static typing, and a rich type system. Influential in programming language research and compiler design.

name: C++
description: Multi-paradigm systems language extending C with classes, templates, and a rich standard library. Dominant in game engines, operating systems, and performance-critical applications.

name: Django
description: High-level Python web framework following the 'batteries included' philosophy. Comes with ORM, admin panel, auth, and templating out of the box.

name: Flask
description: Lightweight Python micro-framework. Minimal core with a rich extension ecosystem. Favoured for APIs and smaller applications that don't need a full framework.

name: FastAPI
description: Modern, async Python web framework for building APIs with automatic OpenAPI docs. Leverages type hints for validation and serialisation via Pydantic.

name: Starlette
description: Lightweight ASGI framework and toolkit for async Python web services. Serves as the foundation of FastAPI.

name: Express.js
description: Minimal, unopinionated Node.js web framework. The de-facto standard for building REST APIs in JavaScript. Large middleware ecosystem.

name: Fastify
description: High-performance Node.js web framework with a plugin system and built-in schema-based serialisation. Significantly faster than Express in benchmarks.

name: NestJS
description: Progressive Node.js framework for building efficient, scalable server-side applications. Uses TypeScript, Angular-style decorators, and a modular architecture.

name: Next.js
description: React framework by Vercel with file-system routing, SSR, static generation, and App Router. The dominant full-stack React solution.

name: Nuxt.js
description: Vue.js meta-framework offering file-system routing, SSR, SSG, and an auto-import system. The Vue equivalent of Next.js.

name: SvelteKit
description: Full-stack framework for Svelte with file-based routing, SSR, and adapter-based deployment. Zero-cost reactivity via compiled components.

name: Astro
description: Content-focused web framework that ships zero JS by default. Islands architecture allows embedding any UI framework (React, Vue, Svelte) selectively.

name: Remix
description: React full-stack framework that embraces web fundamentals (forms, HTTP, browser APIs). Uses loader/action model for data fetching close to the component.

name: Ruby on Rails
description: Full-stack MVC framework for Ruby with ActiveRecord ORM, convention over configuration, and built-in scaffolding. Popularised REST and COC patterns.

name: Laravel
description: Elegant PHP web framework with expressive syntax. Includes Eloquent ORM, Blade templating, queues, broadcasting, and a rich ecosystem.

name: ASP.NET Core
description: Cross-platform, high-performance web framework from Microsoft for building modern APIs and web apps on .NET. Supports MVC, Razor Pages, Minimal APIs, and Blazor.

name: Gin
description: High-performance HTTP web framework for Go. Features zero-allocation router, middleware chain, and JSON validation. Among the fastest Go web frameworks.

name: Fiber
description: Express-inspired Go web framework built on Fasthttp, optimised for ultra-low memory usage and extreme throughput.

name: Echo
description: High-performance, minimalist Go web framework with a focus on clean API design and extensibility. Includes built-in middleware, data binding, and validation.

name: Docker
description: Platform for building, packaging, and running applications in lightweight containers. Standardised the container image format and popularised containerisation.

name: Kubernetes
description: Open-source container orchestration system from Google. Automates deployment, scaling, and management of containerised applications across clusters.

name: Terraform
description: Infrastructure as Code tool by HashiCorp. Declarative HCL language to provision cloud resources across AWS, Azure, GCP, and 1000+ providers.

name: Ansible
description: Agentless IT automation tool using YAML playbooks over SSH. Handles configuration management, application deployment, and orchestration without installing agents.

name: Helm
description: The package manager for Kubernetes. Bundles Kubernetes YAML manifests into versioned, templatable charts that can be shared and deployed consistently.

name: Jenkins
description: Self-hosted, open-source CI/CD automation server. Thousands of plugins cover build, test, and deployment pipelines.

name: GitHub Actions
description: GitHub-native CI/CD platform using YAML workflow files. Tight integration with the GitHub ecosystem, marketplace of reusable actions, and free minutes for open source.

name: ArgoCD
description: Declarative GitOps continuous delivery tool for Kubernetes. Syncs application state from Git repositories to clusters automatically.

name: TensorFlow
description: Open-source ML platform by Google. End-to-end toolkit for building and deploying ML models, from research to production via TF Serving, TF Lite, and TF.js.

name: PyTorch
description: Open-source ML framework by Meta with dynamic computation graphs. Preferred in research for its Pythonic API and dominant in academic publications.

name: scikit-learn
description: Python library for classical machine learning algorithms. Consistent API across classification, regression, clustering, and preprocessing. The ML workhorse for tabular data.

name: Keras
description: High-level neural networks API, now multi-backend (TensorFlow, PyTorch, JAX). Designed for fast prototyping and ease of use while remaining production-ready.

name: Hugging Face Transformers
description: Library of pre-trained transformer models for NLP, vision, and audio. Hub hosts 500k+ models. The standard API for working with LLMs and foundation models.

name: LangChain
description: Framework for building applications powered by large language models. Provides chains, agents, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and tool-calling abstractions.

name: OpenCV
description: Open-source computer vision and image processing library. Covers classical CV algorithms and DNN inference. Available in C++ and Python.

name: SQLAlchemy
description: Python SQL toolkit and ORM. Provides both a Core expression language for raw SQL and a full ORM layer. The de-facto Python ORM for production.

name: Prisma
description: Next-generation TypeScript/JavaScript ORM with a schema-first approach and auto-generated client. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and MongoDB.

name: TypeORM
description: ORM for TypeScript and JavaScript supporting Active Record and Data Mapper patterns. Works with PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, and more.

name: Sequelize
description: Promise-based Node.js ORM for Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, and MSSQL. One of the oldest and most widely used JavaScript ORMs.

name: GORM
description: The fantastic ORM library for Go. Full-featured with associations, hooks, transactions, and auto-migrations. Works with MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and SQL Server.

name: GraphQL
description: Query language for APIs and a runtime for executing those queries. Lets clients request exactly the data they need, avoiding over/under-fetching. Originally from Meta.

name: gRPC
description: High-performance RPC framework from Google using HTTP/2 and Protocol Buffers. Strongly-typed contracts, bi-directional streaming, and multi-language code generation.

name: tRPC
description: Type-safe API layer for TypeScript full-stack apps. Eliminates the API schema layer by sharing types directly between server and client. Zero code generation.

name: OpenAPI
description: Specification standard (formerly Swagger) for describing REST APIs. Machine-readable definitions drive documentation, client SDKs, and server stubs via code generation.

name: AWS CDK
description: AWS Cloud Development Kit. Define cloud infrastructure in TypeScript, Python, Java, or C# using familiar languages instead of raw CloudFormation YAML.

name: Pulumi
description: Infrastructure as Code platform using general-purpose languages (TypeScript, Python, Go, C#). Multi-cloud with a rich provider ecosystem and state management.

name: Serverless Framework
description: CLI-driven framework for building and deploying serverless applications across AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, Google Cloud Functions, and more.

name: Firebase
description: Google's Backend-as-a-Service platform. Provides Firestore (NoSQL), Auth, Hosting, Cloud Functions, Storage, and Realtime Database — all with client SDKs.

name: Supabase
description: Open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL. Provides a managed Postgres database, Auth, Storage, Edge Functions, and auto-generated REST/GraphQL APIs.

name: Jest
description: JavaScript testing framework by Meta with a focus on simplicity. Zero-config setup, built-in code coverage, snapshot testing, and mocking. Standard for React apps.

name: Pytest
description: The dominant Python testing framework. Simple function-based tests, powerful fixtures, parametrize decorator, and a rich plugin ecosystem.

name: Cypress
description: End-to-end testing framework that runs in-browser. Real-time reload, time-travel debugging, automatic waiting, and screenshots/video on failure.

name: Playwright
description: End-to-end testing framework by Microsoft supporting Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. Cross-browser, cross-language (JS, Python, Java, C#), with parallelism and tracing built-in.

name: Vitest
description: Vite-native unit testing framework compatible with Jest's API. Near-instant startup using Vite's ESM pipeline, with first-class TypeScript and JSX support.

name: Vite
description: Next-generation frontend build tool by Evan You. Dev server uses native ES modules (no bundling in dev), production uses Rollup. Fast HMR and minimal config.

name: webpack
description: Powerful, highly configurable JavaScript module bundler. Underpins many production build pipelines. Extensive loader and plugin ecosystem.

name: esbuild
description: Extremely fast JavaScript bundler and minifier written in Go. 10-100x faster than webpack in benchmarks. Powers Vite's dependency pre-bundling stage.

name: Rollup
description: JavaScript module bundler optimised for libraries. Tree-shaking removes unused code, and multiple output formats (ESM, CJS, UMD) support broad compatibility.

name: Bun
description: All-in-one JavaScript runtime, package manager, bundler, and test runner written in Zig. Dramatically faster installs and startup times than Node.js/npm.

name: Amazon Web Services
description: The world's most comprehensive cloud platform by Amazon, with 200+ services covering compute, storage, databases, networking, ML, analytics, security, and more.

name: Microsoft Azure
description: Microsoft's enterprise cloud platform with 200+ services. Strong integration with Windows, Active Directory, and the Microsoft 365 ecosystem. Popular in enterprise environments.

name: Google Cloud Platform
description: Google's cloud platform offering compute, storage, networking, and managed services including BigQuery, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI. Strong in data analytics and ML workloads.

name: DigitalOcean
description: Developer-focused cloud platform with a simple UX and predictable pricing. Offers Droplets (VMs), Kubernetes (DOKS), managed databases, and App Platform.

name: Cloudflare
description: Global CDN, DDoS protection, and edge compute platform. Cloudflare Workers enables serverless at the edge across 300+ PoPs. Also offers DNS, Zero Trust, and R2 storage.

name: AWS Lambda
description: AWS serverless compute service. Runs code in response to events without provisioning servers. Supports Node.js, Python, Java, Go, C#, and custom runtimes. Billed per-invocation.

name: Amazon EC2
description: AWS's flagship virtual server service. Hundreds of instance types optimised for compute, memory, storage, and GPU workloads. Forms the foundation of most AWS architectures.

name: Amazon S3
description: AWS object storage service with 99.999999999% durability. Underpins countless architectures as cheap, infinitely scalable storage for files, backups, data lakes, and static websites.

name: Amazon RDS
description: AWS managed relational database service supporting PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and SQL Server. Handles backups, patching, multi-AZ failover, and read replicas automatically.

name: Amazon DynamoDB
description: AWS fully managed NoSQL database with single-digit millisecond latency at any scale. Supports key-value and document models. Powers many high-traffic AWS-native applications.

name: Amazon Redshift
description: AWS fully managed cloud data warehouse based on PostgreSQL. Columnar storage and MPP architecture deliver fast analytics over petabytes of data. Integrates with S3, Glue, and BI tools.

name: Amazon EMR
description: AWS managed big data platform running Apache Spark, Hive, Presto, HBase, and more on auto-scaling clusters. Processes large datasets from S3 without managing infrastructure.

name: Amazon SageMaker
description: AWS fully managed ML platform covering the entire lifecycle: data labelling, model training, hyperparameter tuning, deployment, and monitoring. Supports all major ML frameworks.

name: AWS CloudFormation
description: AWS-native Infrastructure as Code service using JSON/YAML templates. Provisions and manages AWS resources as stacks with dependency ordering and rollback support.

name: Azure Functions
description: Microsoft's serverless compute service supporting C#, JavaScript, Python, Java, and PowerShell. Triggered by HTTP, queues, timers, blob events, and 20+ other connectors.

name: Azure Kubernetes Service
description: Microsoft's managed Kubernetes service. Handles control plane management, upgrades, scaling, and integrates with Azure AD, Container Registry, and Azure Monitor.

name: Azure DevOps
description: Microsoft's integrated DevOps platform with Boards (Scrum/Kanban), Repos (Git), Pipelines (CI/CD), Test Plans, and Artifacts (package registry) in a single suite.

name: Azure Machine Learning
description: Microsoft's cloud ML platform for building, training, deploying, and monitoring ML models. Supports AutoML, designer (no-code), and SDK-based workflows with MLflow integration.

name: Azure Cosmos DB
description: Microsoft's globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database with single-digit millisecond latency. Supports document, key-value, graph, and column-family models via multiple APIs.

name: Google BigQuery
description: Google's serverless, highly scalable enterprise data warehouse. Supports SQL analytics over petabytes of data in seconds. Built-in ML via BigQuery ML and BI Engine.

name: Google Cloud Run
description: GCP's fully managed serverless container platform. Run any stateless container without managing infrastructure. Auto-scales to zero and supports HTTP and gRPC.

name: Vertex AI
description: Google Cloud's unified ML platform for building, deploying, and scaling ML models. Integrates AutoML, custom training, Model Registry, Feature Store, and Vertex AI Pipelines.

name: Google Kubernetes Engine
description: Google's managed Kubernetes service, the most mature managed K8s offering. Google invented Kubernetes and GKE benefits from the deepest integration with the open-source project.

name: Cloudera
description: Enterprise big data platform (Cloudera Data Platform, CDP) built on Apache Hadoop, Spark, Hive, and Impala. Provides on-premises and hybrid cloud data lakehouse capabilities.

name: Snowflake
description: Cloud-native data platform that separates storage and compute. Delivers a data warehouse, data lake, data sharing, and application development in one platform across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

name: Databricks
description: Unified data analytics and AI platform founded by the creators of Apache Spark. Combines data engineering, data science, and ML on a collaborative lakehouse architecture (Delta Lake).

name: Apache Spark
description: Unified analytics engine for large-scale data processing. Supports batch, streaming, SQL, ML (MLlib), and graph processing. In-memory execution makes it 100x faster than Hadoop MapReduce.

name: Apache Kafka
description: Distributed event streaming platform used as a high-throughput message broker and event log. Backbone of real-time data pipelines and event-driven architectures at massive scale.

name: Apache Hive
description: Data warehouse software built on top of Hadoop that provides SQL-like query language (HiveQL) for analysing large datasets stored in HDFS or S3. Widely used in Hadoop ecosystems.

name: Trino
description: Distributed SQL query engine (formerly PrestoSQL) for running fast, ad-hoc analytics across multiple data sources — S3, HDFS, Hive, databases, and Kafka — without moving data.

name: dbt
description: Data build tool that lets analytics engineers transform data in their warehouse using SQL SELECT statements. Brings software engineering practices (version control, testing, docs) to analytics.

name: Apache Airflow
description: Platform for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring data workflows (DAGs) written in Python. The de-facto standard for data pipeline orchestration.

name: Delta Lake
description: Open-source storage framework that brings ACID transactions, scalable metadata, and time travel to data lakes built on cloud object storage. The storage layer behind Databricks Lakehouse.

name: Apache Iceberg
description: Open table format for huge analytic datasets. Provides ACID transactions, schema evolution, partition evolution, and time travel on top of object storage like S3 and GCS.

name: PostgreSQL
description: The world's most advanced open-source relational database. Full ACID compliance, rich SQL support, JSONB for documents, extensive indexing, and a large extension ecosystem (PostGIS, TimescaleDB).

name: MySQL
description: The world's most popular open-source relational database. Widely used in LAMP stacks, SaaS applications, and e-commerce. Oracle-owned but community edition is freely available.

name: MongoDB
description: The most popular NoSQL document database. Stores data as flexible JSON-like BSON documents. Offers horizontal scaling via sharding, replica sets for HA, and Atlas cloud service.

name: Redis
description: In-memory data structure store used as a cache, message broker, and database. Supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, and pub/sub. Sub-millisecond latency.

name: Elasticsearch
description: Distributed search and analytics engine built on Apache Lucene. The E in the ELK stack (with Logstash and Kibana). Powers full-text search, log analytics, and observability.

name: ClickHouse
description: Open-source column-oriented OLAP database for real-time analytics. Exceptionally fast for analytical queries over billions of rows. Used by major internet companies for clickstream and log data.

name: Apache Cassandra
description: Highly scalable, distributed NoSQL database designed for high write throughput with no single point of failure. Linear scalability across nodes. Used by Netflix, Apple, and Discord.

name: MLflow
description: Open-source platform for managing the ML lifecycle: experiment tracking, model registry, packaging, and serving. Backend-agnostic and works with any ML library.
